steps.title: Use cubic regression to avoid sign errors
steps.content: The main trap is the minus sign before the second parentheses. A fast Desmos method is to evaluate the whole expression at four x-values.

steps.title: Fit the cubic form
steps.content:
Regress the outputs against $ax^3+bx^2+cx+d$:
$$y_1\sim ax_1^3+bx_1^2+cx_1+d$$
Desmos returns $a=7$, $b=0$, $c=0$, and $d=10$.

steps.title: Confirm with the sign rule
steps.content:
Distribute the subtraction:
$$(8x^3+8)-(x^3-2)=8x^3+8-x^3+2=7x^3+10$$
That matches choice B, so B is correct.
